courses.title: Introduction to Statistics
courses.instructor: Bassem A. Saad, John P. Montoya, Patrick T. Mitchell, Caroline L. Dawson, Kyle B. Sugimoto, Lisa A. Diaz, Jeanne M. Witt, Lakhvir K. Atwal, Sean Golbad, Mark R. Sutterfield, Cher Yang
courses.description: Begins Fall 2025. Effective Fall 2025, STAT-C1000 replaces MATH-10 This course is an introduction to statistical thinking and processes, including methods and concepts for discovery and decision-making using data. Topics include descriptive statistics; probability and sampling distributions; statistical inference; correlation and linear regression; analysis of variance, chi-squared, and t-tests; and application of technology for statistical analysis including the interpretation of the relevance of the statistical findings. Students apply methods and processes to applications using data from a broad range of disciplines. Prerequisite: Placement as determined by the college's multiple measuresýassessment process or completion of a course taught at orýabove the level of intermediate algebra.ýPreviously taken ENGL-C1000 (formerly ENGL-01A) is advised.

meta.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Placement as determined by the college's multiple measuresýassessment process or completion of a course taught at orýabove the level of intermediate algebra.

courses.title: Ancient Philosophy
courses.description: Begins Fall 2025 This course presents an introduction to the history of philosophy from the Pre-Socratics to the Renaissance, with emphasis on the Pre-Socratics through Aristotle. Prerequisite: Previously taken ENGL-C1000 (formerly ENGL-01A) is advised.

meta.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Previously taken ENGL-C1000 (formerly ENGL-01A) is advised.

courses.title: Intermediate French II
courses.description: Begins Fall 2025 This course is thorough review of the fundamentals of reading, writing, speaking and understanding French, designed to aid the student in preparing for advanced studies in French composition, grammar, and conversation as well as literature and French, history and culture. Prerequisite: Previously taken FREN-03 ýand passed with a minimum grade of Cýor received an equivalent.ýPreviously taken LRNR-30 is advised.

meta.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Previously taken FREN-03 ýand passed with a minimum grade of Cýor received an equivalent.

courses.title: Intermediate German II
courses.description: Begins Fall 2025 This course is a thorough review of the fundamentals of reading, writing, speaking and understanding German, designed to aid the student in preparing for advanced studies in German composition, grammar, and conversation, as well as literature in German, history and culture. Prerequisite: Previously taken GERN-03 and passed with a minimum grade ofýC or received an equivalent.ýPreviously taken LRNR-30 is advised.

meta.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Previously taken GERN-03 and passed with a minimum grade ofýC or received an equivalent.

courses.title: Comparative Religions
courses.description: Begins Fall 2025 This course surveys the historical background and fundamental philosophical concepts of the major religions of the world, including Hinduism, Buddhism, Shinto, Confucianism, Judaism, Christianity, Islam, and some typical basic religions. Prerequisite: Previously taken ENGL-C1000 (formerly ENGL-01A) is advised.

meta.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Previously taken ENGL-C1000 (formerly ENGL-01A) is advised.

courses.title: Contemporary Ethical Issues
courses.description: Begins Fall 2025 This course examines the concept of morality and values, representative ethical theories, and their application to contemporary ethical issues such as capital punishment, abortion, war, animal rights and economic justice. Prerequisite: Previously taken ENGL-C1000 (formerly ENGL-01A) isýrecommended.

meta.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Previously taken ENGL-C1000 (formerly ENGL-01A) isýrecommended.

courses.title: Introduction to Logic
courses.description: Begins Fall 2025 This course explores symbolic logic, focusing on translating natural language arguments into formal symbolic form and evaluating their validity. Topics include deductive reasoning, truth tables, formal proofs, quantifiers, and rules of inference. Alongside formal exercises, students will write essays analyzing arguments, explaining logical methods, and reflecting on broader applications, with an emphasis on clarity, structure, and revision. Prerequisite: Previously taken ENGL-C1000 (formerly ENGL-01A) is advised.

meta.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Previously taken ENGL-C1000 (formerly ENGL-01A) is advised.

courses.title: Spanish for Spanish Speakers I
courses.description: Begins Fall 2025 This course is designed for native and heritage speakers, as well as other linguistically qualified students, whose formal training in the language is not complete. The course will focus on extensive reading of all types of texts and their reworking in written form with the intention of expanding the vocabulary, creating an incipient awareness of linguistic registers, discussing items beyond the familial routine, improving written expression, and developing an appreciation for Hispanic culture as manifested in Spanish speaking countries and the U.S. This course is entirely conducted in Spanish. Prerequisite: Take SPAN-02 or the equivalent;ýMinimum Grade C,P;ýPreviously taken LRNR-30 is advised.

meta.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Take SPAN-02 or the equivalent;ýMinimum Grade C,P;ýPreviously taken LRNR-30 is advised.

courses.title: Elementary Spanish I
courses.instructor: Alberto E. Zarate, Mara J. Colomer-Flores, Maria D. Gonzalez-Mercado, Natalie M. Sobalvarro-Butler, Silvia, Maria D. Garcia Diaz, Sandra M. Cortes
courses.description: Begins Fall 2025 This course will focus on the development of listening, speaking, reading, and writing in a cultural context, with primary emphasis on communicative competency. Students will learn how to express in Spanish the most basic functions of everyday life. This course is not recommended for native speakers - native speakers should enroll in SPAN-10.

courses.title: Elementary French II
courses.description: Begins Fall 2025 FREN-02 is the continuation of FREN-01. This course will focus on the further development of listening, speaking, reading, and writing in cultural context, with primary emphasis on communicative competency. Students will learn how to express in French basic functions of everyday life. This course is not recommended for native speakers. Prerequisite: Take FREN-01 ý---OR---ýTwo years of high school French.

meta.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Take FREN-01 ý---OR---ýTwo years of high school French.

courses.title: Elementary French I
courses.description: Begins Fall 2025 This is a beginner's course. The course will focus on the development of listening, speaking, reading, and writing in a cultural context, with primary emphasis on communicative competency. Students will learn how to express in French the most basic functions of everyday life. This course is not recommended for native speakers.

courses.title: Elementary German I
courses.description: Begins Fall 2025 This is a beginner's course. The course will focus on the development of listening, speaking, reading, and writing in a cultural context, with primary emphasis on communicative competency. Students will learn how to express in German the most basic functions of everyday life.

courses.title: Elementary German II
courses.description: Begins Fall 2026 GERN-02 is the continuation of GERN-01. This course will focus on the further development of listening, speaking, reading, and writing in a cultural context, with primary emphasis on communicative competency. Students will learn how to express in German basic functions of everyday life. Prerequisite: Previously taken GERN-01 or two years of high school Germanýand passed with a minimum grade of C or received anýequivalent.

courses.title: Elementary Hmong I
courses.description: Begins Fall 2025 This course is a beginner's course. It focuses on the study of the fundamentals of pronunciation, audio-lingual training, and phonology; syllabication; appreciation of basic elements of the Hmong culture, with primary emphasis on communicative competency. Students will learn how to express in Hmong the most basic functions of everyday life.

courses.title: Intermediate German I
courses.description: Begins Fall 2025 GERN 03 is a continuation of GERN 02. This course reviews and further develops grammatical concepts introduced in GERN 01 and GERN 02, as well as introduces the student to new concepts. Through varied readings, composition, and discussion, the student will increase his or her vocabulary and cultural knowledge. Prerequisite: Previously taken GERN-02 and passed with a minimum grade ofýC or received an equivalent.ýPreviously taken LRNR-30 is advised.

meta.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Previously taken GERN-02 and passed with a minimum grade ofýC or received an equivalent.
